title: Will CPI rise more than 0.0% in June 2026?

current.lean_note: 88pt gap exceeds the hard ceiling (20pt) — beyond this divergence from a liquid market, even high confidence is treated as model error, not edge
current.rationale_summary: The evidence strongly supports the likelihood of CPI rising more than 0.0% in June 2026. The May 2026 CPI is projected to rise 4.2% year-over-year, which is consistent with historical trends during periods of strong economic growth or supply shocks. Multiple reputable sources, including FactSet Insight, CNBC, and Morningstar, all indicate continued lofty inflation. These projections are also align
reference_classes: Inflation during periods of economic growth or supply shocks

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 40%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: Local model (0.75) under-weighted the post-ceasefire energy reversal: Hormuz reopened June 14, oil/gas falling, a major drag on June headline MoM after May's gas +40.5% spike. Liquid market prices ~87% chance of flat/negative MoM; deferring substantially toward that mechanism-backed signal while retaining upside from sticky core/shelter.

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 25%, modal NO) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: A positive June headline needs gasoline's SA decline to be far milder than the ~9-13% the primary weekly data implies; sticky core (+0.2%) alone can't offset it, so YES unlikely but above market.
